name: LINE (L)
use: Creates straight line segments between two points. The base tool for almost all 2D layouts.

name: CIRCLE (C)
use: Creates a circle using a center point and a radius or diameter value.

name: RECTANGLE (REC)
use: Creates a rectangular polyline after defining two diagonal corners.

name: FILLET (F)
use: Rounds and fillets the edges of objects, creating a smooth curved corner between two intersecting lines.

name: CHAMFER (CHA)
use: Bevels or cuts a sharp flat angled edge between two intersecting lines instead of rounding it.

name: TRIM (TR)
use: Cuts or trims objects to precisely meet the cutting edges of other intersecting objects. Great for cleaning up messy intersections.

name: EXTEND (EX)
use: Lengthens a line, arc, or open polyline so that it perfectly meets the boundary edge of another object.

name: OFFSET (O)
use: Creates concentric circles, parallel lines, or parallel curves at a specific distance from the original object.

name: MIRROR (MI)
use: Creates a perfectly flipped, mirrored copy of selected geometry across a designated symmetry axis line.

name: MOVE (M)
use: Displaces objects a specified distance in a specified direction from a base point.

name: COPY (CO)
use: Duplicates selected objects and places the copies at specified distance and direction intervals.

name: ROTATE (RO)
use: Turns selected objects around a fixed base point by a specified angular value.

name: SCALE (SC)
use: Enlarges or reduces the uniform size of selected objects equally in all directions while keeping proportions.

name: EXPLODE (X)
use: Breaks a compound object (like a rectangle, block, or polyline) down into its individual, basic component lines or arcs.

name: JOIN (J)
use: Combines a series of individual linear or curved segments that touch endpoints into a single, unified object.

name: Extruded Boss/Base
use: Takes a 2D closed sketch profile and pushes it linearly into a 3D solid feature, adding thickness or depth.

name: Extruded Cut
use: Cuts into or completely removes material from an existing 3D solid model by pushing a 2D sketch profile straight through it.

name: Revolved Boss/Base
use: Rotates a 2D sketch profile around a central centerline axis to create a 3D symmetrical solid shape like a wheel, shaft, or sphere.

name: Revolved Cut
use: Removes material from a 3D part by rotating a closed sketch profile around a central centerline axis to gouge out symmetric channels.

name: Swept Boss/Base
use: Extrudes a 2D profile sketch along a customized, curved 2D or 3D sketch path line. Perfect for pipes, wires, or springs.

name: Lofted Boss/Base
use: Blends multiple distinct 2D sketch profiles on separate parallel planes together into a single smooth 3D shape. Used for organic shapes like car bodies or turbine blades.

name: Fillet
use: Creates a rounded internal or external face on a 3D solid part along selected straight or curved model edges.

name: Chamfer
use: Bevels a sharp 3D model edge by cutting it at a flat angle, defining the look by angle-distance or distance-distance values.

name: Linear Pattern
use: Creates multiple repeating copies of a 3D feature or face arrayed in one or two straight directional paths.

name: Circular Pattern
use: Creates multiple repeating copies of a 3D feature arrayed evenly or at specific angles around a central axis line.

name: Shell
use: Hollows out a solid 3D part, leaving selected faces open and creating thin uniform walls on all remaining sides. Perfect for plastic enclosures.

name: Rib
use: Adds a thin, structural support wall to a 3D model from an open 2D sketch line, usually to reinforce corners or hollow parts.

name: Smart Dimension
use: The universal sketching tool used to define the exact length, radius, angle, or distance constraints of geometry inside a 2D sketch environment.

name: Straight Slot
use: Creates a straight slot feature based on a center line sketch path and a defined width constraint.

name: Line
use: Draws straight line segments contextually inside a 2D sketch profile. The fundamental starting element for most features.

name: Straight Slot
use: Creates a straight slot feature based on a center line sketch path and a defined width constraint.
